reviews.review_text: the game has been abandoned by the devs, the last update was 3 YEARS ago.

You could make a game with the most amazingly handcrafted story with the best combat and beautiful visuals and yet it will never compare to the feeling of everyone in the car collectively pissing and ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ everywhere for no reason while their eyes bulge out of their heads looking at you.

played it
10/10

I bought a steering wheel specifically for this game when I was 24 hours in. Great investment.

this game sucks and I love it so much. I love managing my piss and ♥♥♥♥ while mad maxing my truck and listening to clasical music with occasional ad breaks. never change.

The developer hasn't updated the game in 3 years, rants in the discussions page. Their own words they keep getting burnt out on updating the game and have to completely remake it in Godot because it has been so poorly made that they have to change many things just to change a single thing. I don't see a future were this game ever gets finished. I sadly have purchased 3 copies of this game for me and friends and unfortunately it seems it was a waste.

Do not purchase. 
In all honesty, I love this game. However, I would never recommend this game to anyone who ever would expect any kind of update/ "bug-free" experience/ optimized game. 
The game is dead, the developer has completely restarted development on everything and rewriting the code in Godot. He also spent a year trying to rework the terrain just to remove all of it. His updates are scarce and misleading to give hope. His lack of care for the project is okay in my  books, but leading on people is not. He says he DOES NOT like social interactions, but this seems to only apply to negative news of the project being dead, not to any "possible updates." The game is dead and quite frankly, very annoying to play in multiplayer. The modding community is the only thing that really seems to care about the game. The mods DO NOT work in multiplayer, which is very upsetting but makes sense. 

I still love this game and will continue playing it, but it is not one I would recommend past a niche audience that enjoys dead games. Gene, stop arguing with randoms on forums and leading us on. Tell us the game is dead or show you still care.

I bought 2 copies of this game. One for my son and my self to play multiplayer. The jank was so bad we would end up losing everything a few hours in. Ancient Chinese philosopher Di Liong and Arabic Dr. Aif worked together to create this experience called The Long Drive. In the The Long Drive video game, you drive a ...... long drive.
10/10 - Would recommend to anyone seeking redemption or punishment for their actions, that took place in our ,, earthly journey '' called LIFE.
The 5000km drive can be broken down to three words: Road - Stones - Despair
Wish the game wasn't actually abandoned, but that just strengthens the suffering, hopelessness, and constant urge to shoot your character in the head with the three different firearms in the game!
The game can be a perfect gift for your little nephew or even yourself, if you no longer enjoy the gracious touch of sunlight.

I'd like to state that at the time of posting this, there's discourse whether the game is abandoned, or not. Personally, I believe it is just stuck in development hell, as the next update seems to plan to release a HUGE amount of new random generation, including a whole new highway system. For procedural generation, even if low quality, is an insane task to take on. 

Now, into an actual review of the game: I personally really love "The Long Drive". I keep revisiting the game, creating new builds for my vehicles, and just seeing how far I can get until my short attention span pulls me away to play something else. As you could assume from the name of the game, what you'll mostly be doing is just driving. Every few miles there'll be buildings that generate that you can scavenge (you can even tweak their frequency in the settings, btw. if you want to pillage more often), and while there isn't too much to actually do past vehicle customization and part-swapping, the main-loop of just the drive is more than worth it, in my opinion. 

The lore is pretty bare minimum, but that's not the focus of the game, the drive is. Currently I have a modified motorcycle and am about ⅕th the way through the road. This time I plan to actually reach the end. 

For the time invested, the price seems worth it, and if the future update comes out, the tag will definitely be worth it.
very good game and funny but with mods without mods is very bad to download mods downlod tldworkshop

I gave it a thorough try, over and over, and I had good fun in doing so. But after a while the weaknesses become too evident, too hard to ignore over the benefits. My last straw were the dirty driving physics, and having my car flipped and explode into a million pieces by hitting a bump in the road for the hundredth time. Love the idea for some reason, that seemingly endless, winding road through the desert, stopping in places to have a smoke and scavenge for parts and fuel. But the long drive just doesn't deliver any more, it started great and lost all its momentum. Also the updates are just too damn few and far to take the developers intentions serious at this point. There's better games like this by now.

eu amo muito esse jogo, a proposta dele é simples e perfeita. cê só pega um carro e sai andando numa estrada infinita enquanto ouve música, vê um vídeo, um podcast, ou só joga mesmo. qualquer coisa serve. mas eu tenho um ponto: esse é provavelmente o jogo mais mal otimizado que eu já joguei. ele é muito travado e tem que deixar as configs no low pra rodar bem. pode ser que seja assim porque eu tenho um PC fraco, mas eu rodo jogos muito mais pesados e maiores do que esse sem problemas parecidos, considerando que ele só pesa 500 mega.

conclusão: se você tem um PC bom, compra o jogo, compensa e você vai gostar de passar tempo fazendo nada nele. se você tem um PC abaixo da média, não compra até os Devs arrumarem esses problemas.

After playing The Long Drive by myself and with friends, I have to say it is not worth the purchase. The game is a buggy and unoptimized mess that runs poorly when playing with others. The game is fun to play with friends, yes, but after an hour of playtime, the bugs make it impossible to do anything other than laugh at said absurd bugs. The author/ group who made the game seem to have abandoned the game all together. The last significant update was over a year ago, and there have been no bug fixes in between then and now. There also has not been any communication by the author in almost a year now.

TLDR: abandoned and buggy game; buy on sale if you so want to.

The Long Drive is a game that is fun - for a while.

It's ultimately incomplete and probably wont ever be finished. However, what's there is fun. 

The Long Drive is a post-apoctlyptic driving simulator with a few survival elements mixed in. You start with a random vehicle in a random condition along with a selection of usable items, some random and some fixed. There's one road, and you just drive. You have to worry about things like oil and gas, but also food and water. For the majority of the game, you're just driving along one single road, avoiding the occasional obstacle. There are sometimes buildings with a bit of loot, like food, car parts, weapons, sometimes even coffins, but also a couple of enemies here and there. And that's it.

The enemies are very easy and there's not a lot of variety to the things you can find. The biggest worries are car fluids and food and water.

Car customization is pretty good in this game. You can take any car part and put it on any other car, and it just forces it to be compatible. For example, you can take a diesel engine and put it in a motorcycle or put a box headlight onto any vehicle that doesnt take box lights.

If there was more mod support, or even just content, this game would be absolutely amazing. As it stands now, it's not bad, but it's probably not something that you're going to get 20 or 30 hours out of.
It's a bit buggy, but I think that's what adds to the fun of it all. I've had a blast playing this with my friends!

SO instead of abandoning the game and throwing away the community that has supported this goofy ass game, they instead remake the whole thing and release it as a free update to the original game, all remade in Godot instead of Unity.
